info.name: PlayFab Catalog Service | VMP
info.description:
Postman collection for the PlayFab VMC (View Marketplace) API. It mirrors the current OpenAPI endpoints and keeps the examples practical for local development and manual API checks.

Quick start:
1) Set {{baseUrl}} to your running instance, for example http://localhost:3000.
2) Set {{adminUser}} and {{adminPass}}, then run Auth / POST /login. The token is stored in {{token}} automatically.
3) Replace {{itemId}}, {{friendlyId}}, {{webhookId}}, and player token variables only for requests that need real runtime values.
4) Optional query parameters are included as disabled Postman params on the main requests. Enable only what you need.

Auth model:
The collection uses Bearer {{token}} at collection level. Public endpoints explicitly use no auth.

exec: pm.test("Body parses as JSON", () => { body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body).to.not.equal(undefined); });

request.description: Simple service root response for quick connectivity checks.

request.description: Return service health, version, and uptime details.

exec: pm.test("Body parses as JSON", () => { body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body).to.not.equal(undefined); });

request.description: Fetch the live OpenAPI specification used by Swagger UI.
Open Swagger UI when ENABLE_DOCS=true.

exec: pm.test("Token is present", () => pm.expect(body.token, "Missing token in login response").to.be.a("string").and.not.empty);
try { const payload = JSON.parse(atob(body.token.split(".")[1])); if (payload?.exp) pm.collectionVariables.set("token_exp", payload.exp); } catch (e) {}

request.description: Authenticate and obtain a JWT token for protected endpoints.

item.name: Resolve

exec: pm.test("Body parses as JSON", () => { body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body).to.not.equal(undefined); });
pm.test("Resolved item has an Id when present", () => { if (body && Object.keys(body).length) pm.expect(body.Id || body.id).to.exist; });

query.description: Optional resolve depth. 0 disables recursive resolving; 1 is the API default.
